preview_text:
The model favours Croatia at 48%, while Panama is given a 22% chance of victory. The gap reflects a substantial difference in win probability, though the underdog retains meaningful upside.

The 29.8% draw probability signals a moderately competitive fixture. The 48–22 win split is wide enough to establish a clear favourite, yet the near-30% draw chance suggests the match could plausibly finish level. This indicates competitive balance in the tail, even if Croatia enters as the likely winner.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge noticeably. Books price Croatia 16 percentage points higher (63.9% vs 48%), while assigning Panama 10 percentage points lower (12.7% vs 22.2%). Draw odds also compress in the market to 23.4%. These gaps indicate the market views Croatia as substantially stronger; the dashboard does not adjudicate between the two estimates.
